explicit_text: I chose the United States Air Force Reserve because I liked the Air Force best among the military branches, especially given my mechanical background. I enlisted in December 1964 and went through basic training at Lackland Air Force Base in early 1965. Later, I was part of the 930th Tactical Airlift Group at Bakalar Air Force Base, which was activated during the Vietnam War in 1968. Being in the Reserve allowed me to avoid the draft initially, but eventually, I was called up and deployed to Vietnam as part of a special operations squadron.

explicit_text: I went through my basic training at Lackland Air Force Base in San Antonio, Texas, starting in January 1965. It was a tough six weeks, both mentally and physically, but I got through it by keeping quiet and doing what was expected. All of us in my basic training unit were Air Force reservists, which made the experience somewhat shared. I remember one of my instructors was Sergeant Peace, though I don't recall the other names.

explicit_text: During the Vietnam War, my Air Force Reserve unit was activated in 1968 as part of a larger mobilization announced by the Secretary of Defense. Our unit, the 930th Tactical Airlift Group, was sent to Vietnam to operate new AC-119 gunship aircraft. I was part of the ferry mission that took the aircraft from the U.S. to Vietnam in early 1969, and I served there for about five and a half months. Our mission was to expedite the introduction of these gunships into the conflict and train regular Air Force personnel.

This training was essential for my role as a crew chief later on. I completed tech school in mid-1965 and then returned home to Indianapolis.

explicit_text: From July 1968 until our return in 1969, our unit was known as the 71st Special Operations Squadron. We operated converted C-119 aircraft as gunships, replacing the older AC-47 Spooky gunships in Vietnam. I served as a crew chief in this squadron and participated in the ferry mission to Vietnam in early 1969. Our mission was to introduce and support the AC-119 gunship program in the conflict.
